15.kj: abundance(-antly), all, [idiom] common (sort), excellent, great(-ly, -ness, number), huge, be increased, long, many, more in number, most, much, multitude, plenty(-ifully), [idiom]
[phrase] carbuncle, [phrase] mason, [phrase] plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s).
15.dv: from the root of H1129 (בָּנָה) through the meaning to build;

15.gl: gold, figuratively, something gold-colored (i.e. yellow), as oil, a clear sky
15.dv: from an unused root meaning to shimmer;

15.gl: Lowland, i.e. (with the article) the maritime slope of Palestine

16.gl: a going forth, i.e. (the act) an egress, or (the place) an exit; hence, a source or product; specifically, dawn, the rising of the sun (the East), exportation, utterance, a gate, a fountain, a mine, a meadow (as producin
16.kj: brought out, bud, that which came out, east, going forth, goings out, that which (thing that) is gone out, outgoing, proceeded out, spring, vein, (water-) course (springs).
16.dv: or מֹצָאxlit môtsâ corrected to môtsâʼ; from H3318 (יָצָא);

16.gl: something waited for, i.e. confidence (objective or subjective); also a collection, i.e. (of water) a pond, or (of men and horses) a caravan or drove
16.kj: abiding, gathering together, hope, linen yarn, plenty (of water), pool.
16.dv: or מִקְוֵה; (1 Kings 10:28), or מִקְוֵאlemma מִּקְוֵא extra dagesh, corrected to מִקְוֵא; (2 Chronicles 1:16), from H6960 (קָוָה);

16.gl: to take (in the widest variety of applications)
16.kj: accept, bring, buy, carry away, drawn, fetch, get, infold, [idiom] many, mingle, place, receive(-ing), reserve, seize, send for, take (away, -ing, up), use, win.

16.gl: a horse (as leaping); also a swallow (from its rapid flight)
16.dv: or סֻס; from an unused root meaning to skip (properly, for joy);

16.gl: to travel round (specifically as a pedlar); intensively, to palpitate
16.kj: go about, merchant(-man), occupy with, pant, trade, traffick.

16.dv: from an unused root meaning to buy;

17.gl: a hand (the open one (indicating power, means, direction, etc.),

17.dv: a primitive word; in distinction from H3709 (כַּף), the closed one); used (as noun, adverb, etc.) in a great variety of applications, both literally a

17.gl: to go (causatively, bring) out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
17.kj: [idiom] after, appear, [idiom] assuredly, bear out, [idiom] begotten, break out, bring forth (out, up), carry out, come (abroad, out, thereat, without), [phrase] be condemned, depa

17.gl: to ascend, intransitively (be high) or actively (mount); used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative
17.kj: arise (up), (cause to) ascend up, at once, break (the day) (up), bring (up), (cause to) burn, carry up, cast up, [phrase] shew, climb (up), (cause to, make to) come (up), cut off,

17.gl: Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants; also the name of the son of Shem, a grandson of Nahor, and of an Israelite
17.dv: from the same as H759 (אַרְמוֹן); the highland;

17.gl: a horse (as leaping); also a swallow (from its rapid flight)
17.dv: or סֻס; from an unused root meaning to skip (properly, for joy);

17.gl: six (as an overplus  beyond five or the fingers of the hand); as ord. sixth
17.dv: masculine שִׁשָּׁה; a primitive number; compare H7797 (שׂוּשׂ)
